East Africa

Uganda Travel Guide

Best for travelers who want primates plus classic safari without the price pressure of East Africa’s most premium circuits.

General information about Uganda

Uganda is one of Africa’s best-value wildlife countries, blending gorilla trekking, chimpanzee tracking, Nile landscapes, and savannah safari parks into one route.

Getting around

Access is strongest through Entebbe with onward road circuits and selective domestic flights into southwestern or northern safari sectors.

Best time to visit

Dryer months are usually easiest for gorilla trekking trails and game viewing, though Uganda remains rewarding year-round thanks to its mix of forest and river-based experiences.

Planning notes

Use this country page to compare attractions, understand how to move between them, and decide whether the trip works best as a standalone route or part of a broader regional circuit.

Country at a glance

Uganda

  • 2 featured attractions seeded
  • 1 active tour operator profiles
  • 2 accommodations nearby
  • 2 recommended restaurants

Attractions

Tourist attractions in Uganda

Each listing opens into a full independent attraction page with gallery, practical information, how to get there, nearby stays, restaurants, and booking links.

Uganda

Bwindi Impenetrable National Park

Mountain gorilla trekking in one of Africa’s most powerful forest landscapes.

★ 4.9 (842) From $700
Southwestern Uganda
View Details

Uganda

Murchison Falls National Park

Uganda’s flagship Nile-and-savannah park with boat safaris, game drives, and the dramatic falls.

★ 4.7 (615) From $45
Northern Uganda
View Details
